metadata.summary: Layered testing pyramid: unit (many), integration (some), contract (cross-team), E2E (few). Negative coverage mandatory. Spec-conformance validation closes spec/code gap.

content.title: API Testing
content.summary: Structured methodology for verifying HTTP/gRPC/GraphQL API correctness using a layered pyramid: unit tests (many), integration tests (some), contract tests (consumer-driven, for cross-team interfaces), and E2E tests (few). Every endpoint must have at least one negative test (missing auth, wrong role, malformed body, rate limit).

why.p: APIs break across teams when consumer and provider deploy independently. Contract tests catch schema drift before production. Spec-conformance validation (openapi-core, Schemathesis) closes the gap between what the spec says and what the code does. Without negative cases, auth bypasses and validation gaps ship undetected.

list.item: Any HTTP/gRPC/GraphQL service heading toward production. The pyramid (unit → integration → contract → e2e) applies.
Cross-team interfaces where consumer and provider deploy independently — contract tests (Pact / Spring Cloud Contract / pact-broker) catch drift.
API published with an OpenAPI / GraphQL / Protobuf spec — automatic spec-conformance tests close the spec/code gap.
Public SaaS API where SLA includes correctness + behavior (rate limit, idempotency, error shape).
Refactor / version bump — regression suite is the safety net.
Throwaway prototype. Smoke test with curl, ship.
When the test costs more to maintain than the bug it would catch (low-traffic admin endpoint with no consumers).
Replacing static type checks with runtime tests — let TypeScript / mypy / pydantic catch what they can.
100%-coverage cargo cult — coverage is a leading indicator, not a goal.

rule.statement: Structure API tests in four layers: many unit tests (pure logic), some integration tests (DB + service boundaries), fewer contract tests (consumer-provider interfaces), fewest E2E tests (full-stack flows).
rule.rationale: Each layer has a different cost/confidence tradeoff. Inverting the pyramid (many E2E, few unit) produces a slow, brittle suite that nobody runs locally.

rule.statement: Tag tests by tier and run them on separate CI triggers: unit on every push, integration on every PR, E2E nightly and pre-deploy.
rule.rationale: Fast feedback for the common case; full coverage before deploy without blocking developer iteration.

rule.statement: Every endpoint must have at minimum these negative cases: missing Authorization header (401), insufficient role/scope (403), malformed or missing required body field (422), and rate limit exceeded (429).
rule.rationale: Auth bypasses and validation gaps are the most common API bug class. A green happy-path suite provides false confidence if negative cases are absent.

rule.statement: For POST endpoints that allow client retries, include an idempotency test: two identical calls with the same Idempotency-Key must produce the same result without duplicating side effects.
rule.rationale: Idempotency failures cause duplicate orders, charges, and records in production — they are invisible in a single-call test suite.

rule.statement: Validate every response against the OpenAPI or GraphQL schema using openapi-core or a schema-validation assertion — do not rely solely on status code checks.
rule.rationale: Schema drift introduced by refactors or agent edits passes status-code assertions while silently breaking downstream consumers.

rule.statement: Drive test case generation from the spec (openapi.yaml), not from the implementation. For each operation: derive positive payload from the spec example, derive negative payloads by dropping required fields or using wrong types.
rule.rationale: Tests derived from implementation assert what the code does, not what the spec requires. Spec-derived tests catch regressions that code-derived tests miss.

rule.statement: Create test data via factories (factory_boy, polyfactory, fishery) — never inline literals in multiple tests. Reset the database between tests using transactions, not TRUNCATE.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded literals diverge across test copies and mask order-dependent bugs. TRUNCATE is slow and hides isolation failures.

rule.statement: For tests that call third-party APIs, record and replay HTTP cassettes (vcrpy, pytest-recording). Re-record on a schedule and diff for surprises.
rule.rationale: Live third-party calls introduce flakiness, cost, and test-environment pollution. Cassettes make tests deterministic and offline-capable.

example.title: Pact Consumer Contract Test
example.note: Consumer defines the interaction: what request it will send and what response shape it expects. Pact generates a pact file for the provider to verify.

example.title: FastAPI Integration Test with Negative Cases
example.note: Tests cover 201 happy path, 404 not-found, and 400 validation error — all in one class, sharing auth fixture.

example.title: OpenAPI Response Validation
example.note: Validates that the actual response body matches the schema declared in openapi.yaml — catches drift that status-code assertions miss.
example.code:
from openapi_core import OpenAPI from openapi_core.testing.mock import MockRequest, MockResponse
spec = OpenAPI.from_file_path("openapi.yaml")
def test_list_users_matches_spec(client, auth_headers): response = client.get("/users", headers=auth_headers) mock_req = MockRequest("http://localhost", "GET", "/users") mock_resp = MockResponse(data=response.content, status_code=response.status_code) result = spec.validate_response(mock_req, mock_resp) assert not result.errors, f"Schema violations: {result.errors}"

antipattern.description: Asserting only status code: assert response.status_code == 200
antipattern.reason: Does not catch body schema drift, missing required fields, or wrong types. Add validate_response(spec, response) and at least one body field assertion.

antipattern.description: Hardcoded primary keys like user_id="1" shared across test functions
antipattern.reason: Collides across parallel test runs and masks order-dependent bugs. Use factories that generate unique values.

section.name: failure-modes-and-limitations
section.p: Slow integration suites (real DB, real external APIs) become the thing nobody runs locally → tests rot.
Flaky tests caused by time, randomness, network, or shared state poison the signal. One flake destroys trust in 100 green runs.
Mocks drift from reality: tests pass, prod breaks. Contract tests + spec validation guard against this.
E2E suites against staging are quickly invalidated by data resets / shared environments.
Test data setup is often longer than the assertion → people copy-paste and tests diverge. Use factories/fixtures.
Auth + rate-limit + idempotency interactions are easy to forget — most bug reports are in this combinatorial gap.
LLM-generated tests often assert what the code does, not what the spec requires — they "pass" while hiding bugs.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Drive tests from the spec, not from the implementation. The agent reads the OpenAPI / GraphQL schema, generates positive + negative cases per operation (auth, validation, rate-limit, idempotency), runs them, then proposes contract pacts for downstream consumers. SDD gate enforces spec-conformance + at least one negative test per endpoint. For external APIs, agents record HTTP cassettes (VCR / pytest-recording) so re-runs are deterministic offline.

list.item: Recommended subagent: faion-feature-executor — sequence: read spec → generate cases → run → record cassettes → commit.
Recommended subagent: faion-sdd-execution — quality gate enforcing happy + 401 + 403 + 422 + 429 cases per endpoint.
A contract-tester agent (Sonnet) — wires Pact consumer + provider verification into CI.
A flake-detective agent (Sonnet) — runs the suite N times, classifies failures, proposes deterministic fixes.

section.code: Read openapi.yaml. For each operation in tags=[users, orders], generate a pytest function in tests/contract/test_<resource>.py that asserts: - 200/201 happy path with example payload from spec - 401 when Authorization missing - 403 when role lacks scope (use fixture `low_priv_token`) - 422 with malformed body (drop one required field) - 429 after 11 calls in 1s (rate limit) Use openapi-core for response-schema validation. Do not invent endpoints. Output: unified diff only.

Run `pytest -p no:randomly --reruns 3 -q tests/integration` 5 times. Identify any test that failed at least once. Output a table: test_id | failure_count | suspected_cause | proposed_fix. Do NOT modify tests, only diagnose.
section.p: Schemathesis fuzz against a running app (CI gate):
section.code: #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail APP=${1:?usage: $0 http://localhost:8000} schemathesis run "$APP/openapi.json" \ --checks all \ --hypothesis-max-examples=50 \ --workers 4 \ --header "Authorization: Bearer $TEST_TOKEN" \ --junit-xml=reports/schemathesis.xml

list.item: Pyramid: many unit tests (pure logic), some integration (DB + service), few contract (consumer-driven), fewest e2e.
Negative cases are required, not nice-to-have. Per endpoint: missing auth, wrong role, malformed body, missing required field, rate limit, idempotency.
Validate every response against the OpenAPI / GraphQL schema with a generic assertion — it catches drift agents introduce.
Test data via factories (factory_boy, polyfactory, fishery) — never inline literals in 50 tests.
Reset DB between tests with transactions or per-test schemas; never with `truncate` (slow + masks order bugs).
Tag tests by tier; CI runs unit on every push, integration on PR, e2e nightly + pre-deploy.
Record + replay cassettes for tests that hit third-party APIs. Re-record on schedule, diff for surprises.
Idempotency: every POST that allows retries must be tested with two identical calls + Idempotency-Key.
Rate limit tests use a knob to lower the limit in test config; do not actually hit prod limits.
Property-based tests (Hypothesis, Schemathesis) find inputs you didn't think of — run on the spec.

rule.statement: Force agents to assert against openapi.yaml, not against current implementation. Prompt: "assert against the spec, not the code."
rule.rationale: Agents default to writing tests that mirror current behavior. Tests pass; regressions slip because the spec was never consulted.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated tests pile up time.sleep(1) to wait for async operations
antipattern.reason: Creates slow, flaky tests. Replace with explicit polling helpers or pytest-asyncio fixtures.

antipattern.description: Agent fixing a flaky test by adding --reruns without a root-cause fix
antipattern.reason: Hides the real problem. Require any --reruns > 0 to have an issue link explaining the flake.

antipattern.description: Agent commits production secrets into test config files
antipattern.reason: Test files often skip linting. Run secret-scan on tests/ too.

antipattern.description: E2E agent suite against shared staging mutates data for other tests
antipattern.reason: Use per-run namespacing (org_id derived from PR number) or ephemeral environments.

list.item: schemathesis — property-based testing of OpenAPI/GraphQL APIs; pip install schemathesis
dredd — OpenAPI conformance runner; npm i -g dredd
pact-cli — contract broker verifier; docs.pact.io
newman — Postman collection runner for CI; npm i -g newman
hurl — plain-text HTTP test files, fast in CI; brew install hurl
k6 — load + functional checks; k6.io
prism — OpenAPI mock + validating proxy; npm i -g @stoplight/prism-cli
vcrpy / pytest-recording — record/replay HTTP cassettes; pip install pytest-recording
PactFlow (SaaS) — hosted Pact broker + can-i-deploy; REST API, agent-friendly
Checkly (SaaS) — synthetic monitoring + Playwright; checkly CLI + IaC
Stoplight Prism (OSS) — OpenAPI mock + validating proxy; agent-drivable
WireMock Cloud (SaaS + OSS) — mock external APIs; JSON config
42Crunch (SaaS) — OpenAPI security audit + conformance; CI plugin

template.purpose: pytest fixtures for auth, db, and factory-based test data
Schemathesis fuzz CI gate against a running OpenAPI app
Agent prompt: generate per-endpoint test cases from OpenAPI spec
